recipes.label: The "Even my picky bf will eat it" Salad
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Wash and cut romaine lettuce and divide between two bowls.

Step 2: Chop bell pepper and spread evenly between the two salads.

Step 3: Make the dressing by blending the ripe avocado with the lime juice, Tabasco, cilantro, 2 tablespoons olive oil, and water (start with 1/4 cup and add more until the desired consistency is reached. We kept it pretty thick.) Season with salt and pepper to taste.

Step 4: Heat a skillet over medium heat.

Step 5: Add the remaining tablespoon oil. Then add the minced garlic. Once the garlic is fragrant, add the shrimp.

Step 6: Once the shrimp is cooked through, add it to the salads. Divide the dressing between the two salads too.

Step 7: Sprinkle cashews on top if desired.

Step 8: Serve with tortilla chips.

recipes.label: salmon fried rice
ingredients: 2 green onions, thinly sliced, including the greens
4 cups day old cooked white rice (from about 2 cups of raw rice)
2 cups cooked salmon in large chunks
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Dissolve the brown sugar in the soy sauce in a small bowl. Set aside.2

Step 2: Heat the olive oil in a large sauté pan or wok on medium high heat.

Step 3: Add the diced red onion and bell pepper and sauté until lightly browned, about 5 minutes. Lower the heat to medium and stir in the garlic and grated ginger. Cook for a minute more. 3

Step 4: Add the beaten eggs and stir until just cooked.

Step 5: Add the cooked rice, increase the heat to medium high and cook for a couple minutes more, stirring often.

Step 6: Add the peas, salmon, and green onions. (Be gentle with the salmon so you don't break it up too much.) Stir in the soy sauce mixture and remove from heat.

Step 7: Garnish with cilantro to serve, serve immediately.

recipes.label: Quinoa Salad with Barberries & Nuts
ingredients: 2 tbsp olive oil (not extra-virgin, it will overpower other flavours)
A few sprigs of mint, chopped
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Put the quinoa in a sieve and rinse under running water.

Step 2: Drain.

Step 3: Pour the boiling water over quinoa in a medium saucepan.

Step 4: Add salt, stir well and cook over medium-low heat until quinoa is cooked through but still has a bite in the center.

Step 5: Drain in a sieve.Pick over the barberries, rinse in a sieve and dry on kitchen paper.While the quinoa is cooking, heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a saucepan over medium heat and lightly toast the almond slivers.

Step 6: Add the slivered pistachios, then the rest of the oil and the rinsed barberries. Cook briefly until the barberries are shiny and a little puffed.Save a tablespoon of the barberry and nut mix and a tablespoon of the chopped herbs for garnishing the finished dish.

Step 7: Mix all of the remaining nut and barberry mix, herbs and quinoa. Return to the pot and place on low heat. Cover with a lid and cook for ten minutes or until completely heated through and steam is rising. Turn into a serving dish and fluff with a fork.

Step 8: Add a little extra olive oil and a twist of black pepper if you wish.

Step 9: Sprinkle the quinoa with the rest of the berry-nut mix and chopped herbs and serve on its own as a main dish or as a side dish.

scenario.description: Derek's vegan breakfast — low calorie, lose weight goal

recipes.label: Cranberry-Ginger Oatmeal With Toasted Hazelnuts
ingredients: 1 inch piece of fresh, peeled ginger root
recipes.instructions: Step 1: Add water, cranberries, ginger, and spices to a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat. When the cranberries begin to burst, lower the heat to medium-low and add the oats, stirring occasionally until the desired consistency is reached, about 5-7 minutes. When oatmeal is cooked, remove the ginger and cinnamon stick and add salt.Meanwhile, heat a small skillet over medium-low heat and add hazelnuts. Toast in the pan until you can smell the hazelnuts, as this means they are releasing their oils. Turn off heat and add hazelnuts to the oatmeal.

recipes.label: Vegan Cacao Crunch Granola
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Preheat the oven to 150C. Line a flat baking tray with parchment paper.In a saucepan on a low heat, add the maple syrup and coconut oil and stir until the coconut oil is completely melted.

Step 2: Add the oats and almonds into a large bowl and pour the maple syrup and coconut oil mixture over. Stir so everything is evenly coated.

Step 3: Add the cacao powder and stir again.

Step 4: Lay the mixture flat on the baking tray and spread out so it's all in one layer.

Step 5: Bake for 45 minutes, stirring every 10-15 minutes to ensure an even cook. Once done, remove from the oven and allow to cool.Once it is completely cool, add to a bowl and mix in the puffed quinoa. Store in a glass jar for up to 2 months.

recipes.label: Country Breakfast: Tofu and Veggie Scramble With Home Fries
ingredients: 3 inches red potatoes, diced ½ thick
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Tofu and veggie scramble

Step 2: Press the tofu for 30 minutes prior to cooking. Chop all the veggies and leave ready to begin cooking process.

Step 3: Using a non-stick skillet, heat olive oil on medium heat.

Step 4: Add pressed tofu and crumble in large pieces. The tofu will need to cook and dry out a little for 10 to 15 minutes, so meanwhile, add onions and garlic to tofu.

Step 5: After those 15 minutes, add asparagus and mushroom and all the rest of the spices plus the water. Cook for 5 minutes and serve hot!

Step 6: Home Fries

Step 7: Prepare potatoes before starting on tofu scramble.

Step 8: Preheat oven to 450F and move oven rack to the next to last highest row. Line a small cookie sheet with parchment paper.

Step 9: Wash and scrub potatoes. Dice them and add to a medium bowl.

Step 10: Add olive oil, salt and pepper and mix well.

Step 11: Pour potatoes on cookie sheet and spread evenly.

Step 12: Bake for 20 minutes and serve while hot.

scenario.description: Derek's vegan lunch — dislikes coconut and spicy food

recipes.label: Lentils and Apples with Acorn Squash
ingredients: 2 medium acorn squash, halved and seeds scraped out
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: In a medium sized saucepan, stir together the first nine ingredients.

Step 2: Add the lentils and mix well.

Step 3: Bring to a boil, cover, turn the heat down and let simmer for about an hour, stirring a few times and tossing the diced apple into the saucepan after the first 20 minutes. Check to see if the lentils are soft and the liquid has thickened. If too liquidy, let simmer uncovered near the end. The lentils should have the consistency of a thick sauce.

Step 4: While the lentils are cooking, preheat oven to 400 degrees.

Step 5: Place the 4 squash halves in a roasting dish (cut a small slice off the bottom of each if needed so they sit securely) and add about an inch of water to the pan. Cover with foil and place in the oven. Steam the squash until tender, about 30-45 minutes depending upon how large they are.

Step 6: Remove from oven and set aside until lentils are cooked.

Step 7: Place the squash halves on a serving platter and season to taste with salt and pepper. Spoon the lentils into each half and top with a sprig of parsley.

Step 8: If making ahead: Prepare the squash and lentils and keep separate. When close to serving, spoon the lentils into the squash and warm in the oven.

recipes.label: Indian Lentil Dahl
ingredients: 1 t. Garam Masala (can be easily found in the supermarket spice isle)
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: In a large saucepan, combine oil, onion and salt at medium-high heat. Cook until onion starts to brown, around 5 minutes or so.

Step 2: Add garlic, ginger and Garam Masala , cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.

Step 3: Add lentils, coconut milk and water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer, partially covered until lentils are fully tender and broken down to an almost paste-like consistency, about 25-30 minutes.

Step 4: Add lime juice and cilantro. Check for seasoning.

Step 5: Serve (over rice if you like), topped with the diced tomatoes.

recipes.label: Tomato and lentil soup
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Saut onion and garlic in olive oil for 5 minutes.

Step 2: Add the carrot, saut for another 2 minutes.

Step 3: Add tomatoes, bay leaf and water, stir and bring to the boil.

Step 4: Stir in lentils, season with salt and cook for 5 minutes.

Step 5: Before serving sprinkle with chopped parsley.

scenario.description: Derek's dinner — vegan + dislikes combined (tests fallback relaxation)

recipes.label: Black Bean and Sweet Potato Enchiladas with Avocado Cream Sauce
ingredients: 1/3 cup black olives, pitted and roughly chopped
1/2 red bell pepper, seeded and chopped
1 red chili, seeded and finely chopped (or a pinch of cayenne)
1/2 to 1 teaspoon sea salt, or to taste
1 teaspoon sea salt, or to taste
2 large handfuls of spinach, roughly chopped
2 tablespoons chickpea flour (besan) or unbleached white flour
2 cups cooked black beans (2/3 cup dried or 1 19 oz can)
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: To make the enchilada sauce, heat the oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. When hot, toss in the shallot, garlic and chili if using and saut for a few minutes. Stir in the flour until the vegetables are coated. Now stir in the chili powder, cumin, paprika and cayenne if using. Stir for another minute.

Step 2: Add the nutritional yeast and fresh tomato, whisk the mixture, and simmer a few minutes. Stir in the tomato paste, stock and salt. Simmer for another 5 minutes or until thickened.

Step 3: Add a bit more stock if the sauce is too thick. It should be creamy and thick, but not watery. Set aside until it's time to assemble the enchiladas.To boil the sweet potato, peel and cut into small pieces and boil in enough water to cover until fork tender  about 5 to 7 minutes. Or to roast the sweet potato, preheat an oven to 400, pierce the potato a few times with a fork, and bake until fork tender  about 30 minutes.

Step 4: Let cool, remove the skin, and chop into small pieces.To make the enchilada filling, heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. When hot, add the onion and saut for 5 minutes.

Step 5: Add the garlic and jalapeo and continue to saute for another few minutes. Stir in the chili powder and cumin.Now add the red pepper, sweet potato, black beans and rice and stir well to combine.

Step 6: Add the spinach, a small handful at a time, and cook over medium-high heat until the spinach is wilted.

Step 7: Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in the olives, 1/2 cup of the enchilada sauce, lime juice and salt.Preheat an oven to 35

Step 8: To assemble the enchiladas, grease a large 9 x 13 baking dish with olive oil.

Step 9: Spread about 1 cup of the enchilada sauce over the bottom of the dish. Scoop about 3/4 cup of the filling onto each tortilla, wrap, and place seam-side down into the dish. Top with any remaining filling and the rest of the sauce.

Step 10: Bake for 20 to 25 minutes until the sauce has thickened and is a deep red colour.While the enchiladas are baking, prepare the avocado cream sauce. In a small food processor or blender, mince the cilantro.

Step 11: Add the avocado, water, lime juice and salt. Process until well combined and creamy, adding more water as necessary.To serve, individually plate the enchiladas and drizzle each with avocado cream sauce.

recipes.label: Sweet Potato, Kale & White Bean Soup
ingredients: 1 medium sweet potato, peel on, chopped into ½” cubes
1 head red kale, stems removed, leaves chopped
½ cup white wine (I used chardonnay)
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: In a large pot, heat the grapeseed oil over medium high and add the sweet potato. Saut the sweet potato, stirring consistently about 5 minutes before adding the onion. Saut about 8 minutes then add a splash of chicken broth to help steam the sweet potato and onion (the chicken broth should sizzle when it hits the pot). Continue cooking until sweet potato is softened but still al dente, another 5 minutes or so.

Step 2: Add all of the chicken broth, white wine, cannellini beans and the oregano and thyme. Stir well and bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium low and simmer covered about 10 minutes.

Step 3: Add the chopped kale leaves, stir, cover again and cook another 5 minutes until kale leaves are softened. Taste the soup and add salt and ground black pepper to taste.

recipes.label: Easy Roasted Vegetables
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Preheat oven to 200 degrees Celsius (fan: 180 degrees Celsius)

Step 2: Peel and cut the vegetables into bite size pieces (the potatoes dont necessarily need to be peeled).

Step 3: Mix the olive oil, paprika, dried basil, salt and pepper in a large bowl before adding the vegetables.

Step 4: Mix everything together well.

Step 5: Place the vegetables on a large baking tray/baking dish. Make sure they are spread out evenly. Aim for single layer.

Step 6: Cook the vegetables in the oven for about 30 minutes. They should be tender but still have a slight bite to them.

recipes.label: Peanut Butter And Chocolate Oatmeal
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Microwave all ingredients in a small bowl or mug for 1 minute, stir and enjoy!

Step 2: Serve with crushed peanuts or almonds for some extra crunch!

recipes.label: Easy Berry French Toast
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Beat eggs.

Step 2: Add milk and vanilla sugar.Dip the bread into the egg mixture one slice at a time, lightly pressing the slices into the egg mixture. Turn to coat both sides evenly. Melt 1 tbsp of butter in a large skillet over medium heat.

Step 3: Add 2-3 slices to the skillet (depending on the size of your bread and your skillet).Fry the bread until browned on both sides.Keep warm in the oven while you finish the other slices, adding butter to the pan as needed.Top with fresh or heated frozen berries (I heat frozen berries gently in a saucepan while I make the French toast).

Step 4: Serve with maple syrup.

recipes.label: Chicken Parmesan With Pasta
ingredients: 2 breasts of chicken, cut in half, into two fillets
1 cup flour, season with salt and pepper
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Preheat oven to 350.Start with cutting your chicken breasts into halves. Season both sides of your chicken with salt and pepper, then dredge each breast in flour. Be sure to coat each side and the edges.

Step 2: Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.In a small sauce pan, warm your pasta sauce.Lightly beat 1 egg in a bowl. Put about 1 cup of bread crumbs in another bowl and season with a little garlic powder. Take your floured chicken and dip into the egg wash, coating all over, then into the bread crumbs. Bread all four chicken breasts.

Step 3: Put the chicken into the skillet and cook for three minutes on each side, or until golden brown on each side.

Step 4: Remove the chicken to a baking sheet. Finish the chicken in the 350 degree oven for 10-15 minutes. {Depending on how thick your chicken breasts, add more or less time.}In a large pot, boil the water needed for your pasta. We used angel hair which only takes 3-5 minutes to cook. But if youre using a penne or something denser youll need more time for the pasta.Pull the chicken out and top with mozzarella cheese, put back into the oven to melt the cheese.Plate the pasta first, topped with chicken and the cover with sauce. If youre feeling zesty add a little fresh shaved Pecorino-Romano or grated Parmesan cheese!

recipes.label: Ratatouille Pasta
ingredients: 3 cups Zucchini, peeled, thinly sliced(⅛ of an inch), cut into bite size pieces
3 cups Eggplant, peeled, thinly sliced(⅛ of an inch), cut into bite size pieces
14 ounces can of Artichokes, rinsed, chopped into bite size pieces
Extra Virgin Olive oil, for serving
Fresh grated Parmesan or Romano, for serving
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Saute the yellow onion in a large skillet or saute pan with enough Olive oil to coat the bottom of the pan, over medium-low heat until tender and translucent. Season with salt and pepper.

Step 2: Add garlic, saute until fragrant.

Step 3: Add another tablespoon of Olive oil and the zucchini, eggplant and Italian seasoning. Continue sauteing until the zucchini and eggplant start to break down and become limp and tender.

Step 4: Add the artichokes and tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper. Bring to a simmer for ten - fifteen minutes. Taste and re-season if needed.

Step 5: Meanwhile cook the spaghetti to al dente according to package directions.

Step 6: Toss with pasta and serve with a drizzle of extra-virgin Olive oil and Parmesan cheese.

recipes.label: Italian Tuna Pasta
ingredients: 250 grams of pasta shells (cook pasta according to packet's instructions)
400 grams of tuna, drained and flaked
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Once pasta is cooked, drain and leave to cool for a minute.

Step 2: Place small skillet on medium fire, drizzle olive oil, add in red pepper and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes. Put aside.Toss pasta shells, red pepper, tuna, parsley, garlic, chillies and lemon juice. Season with ground black pepper to taste, spoon into serving bowls.

recipes.label: Colorful Tomato and Spinach Seafood Pasta
ingredients: 8 ounces cod, cut into bite sized pieces
1/4 cup to ½ basil (dependent on your taste), roll leaves together and then slice into thin pieces
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Cook spaghetti according to package directions.

Step 2: Heat oil in large non-stick skillet.

Step 3: Add onion and garlic and cook 3-4 minutes.

Step 4: Add tomatoes and cook another 3-4 minutes until tender.Toss cod with flour and salt and pepper to taste.

Step 5: Add cod to pan and cook another 3 minutes, add another tsp olive oil if necessary.

Step 6: Add chicken broth and wine and cook another 3 minutes, stirring gently to make sauce, but not break up fish.

Step 7: Add spinach and basil and cook another 3-4 minutes until spinach just wilts and sauce has reduced nicely.Top pasta with cod and parmesan cheese.

recipes.label: Ratatouille Pasta
ingredients: 3 cups Zucchini, peeled, thinly sliced(⅛ of an inch), cut into bite size pieces
3 cups Eggplant, peeled, thinly sliced(⅛ of an inch), cut into bite size pieces
14 ounces can of Artichokes, rinsed, chopped into bite size pieces
Extra Virgin Olive oil, for serving
Fresh grated Parmesan or Romano, for serving
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Saute the yellow onion in a large skillet or saute pan with enough Olive oil to coat the bottom of the pan, over medium-low heat until tender and translucent. Season with salt and pepper.

Step 2: Add garlic, saute until fragrant.

Step 3: Add another tablespoon of Olive oil and the zucchini, eggplant and Italian seasoning. Continue sauteing until the zucchini and eggplant start to break down and become limp and tender.

Step 4: Add the artichokes and tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper. Bring to a simmer for ten - fifteen minutes. Taste and re-season if needed.

Step 5: Meanwhile cook the spaghetti to al dente according to package directions.

Step 6: Toss with pasta and serve with a drizzle of extra-virgin Olive oil and Parmesan cheese.

recipes.label: Pasta With Italian Sausage
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Crumble sausage into a large skillet. Cook over medium heat, stirring often, until meat is well browned.

Step 2: Drain and set aside. In same skillet, cook onion, pepper, garlic, basil and fennel in 1 tb olive oil over medium heat, stirring often to scrape up browned bits from the bottom of pan. Cook until onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.

Step 3: Add sausage, pasta sauce, and wine. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer 5 minutes. While sauce is cooking, in a large pot of boiling water cook pasta until al dente.

Step 4: Drain well.On a large warm serving platter, combine parsley and 1 tb olive oil & fresh basil.

Step 5: Add pasta and toss to mix.

Step 6: Pour sauce over pasta.

Step 7: Sprinkle with Parmesean cheese.

recipes.label: Corn Bread
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.

Step 2: Generously grease a 9 x 13 inch baking pan or a 12 inch cast iron skillet.

Step 3: Sift the dry ingredients into a large bowl.

Step 4: In another bowl, combine the wet ingredients and stir until mixed.

Step 5: Fold the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients.

Step 6: Smooth the batter into the baking pan or skillet and bake for 25 to 30 minutes, or until a knife inserted into the center comes out clean.

Step 7: Allow the corn bread to cool for at least 10 minutes before serving.

scenario.description: Frank's lunch — must stay under budget after breakfast

recipes.label: Hungarian Beef Goulash
ingredients: 1 ounce x 9 package frozen artichoke hearts thawed
1 1/2 pounds beef stew meat cut into 1 inch cubes
1 ounce x 14 can chunky tomatoes with garlic and 1 ounce x 6 can tomato paste
1 teaspoon caraway seed or fennel seed
3 mediums potatoes cut into 1 inch cubes
1In a 3 1/2, 4 or 5 quart crockery cooker, place potatoes, onions and garlic.
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: In a 3 1/2, 4 or 5 quart crockery cooker, place potatoes, onions and garlic.

Step 2: Add beef. In a bowl combine broth, undrained tomatoes, tomato paste, paprika, caraway or fennel seed and salt.

Step 3: Pour over all. cover; cook on low heat setting for 10-12 hours or on high heat setting for 5-6 hours. Stir thawed artichoke hearts into cooker. Cover and let stand for 10 minutes.

Step 4: Serve over hot cooked noodles. Top each serving with a spoonful of sour cream.Makes 6 servings.

recipes.label: Shredded Roast Beef Stuffed Sweet Potatoes (Whole 30 & PALEO)
recipes.instructions: Step 1: Place the roast in Crock Pot, cover with beef broth,  and cook on low for 8 hours. Fully cook the sweet potatoes when ready. When roast is finished, shred and serve over the sweet potatoes.

recipes.label: Mexican Stuffed Potatoes
ingredients: 8 tablespoons low fat cheddar cheese, shredded
1 cup roma tomato, seeded and chopped
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: Pierce potatoes and microwave the potatoes on high from 15  20 minutes until fork tender.

Step 2: In a medium sized non-stick skillet over medium heat, cook hamburger until no longer pink, then add salsa and heat through.

Step 3: Slice potatoes lengthwise and squish apart. Gently smash the insides with a fork.

Step 4: Top each potato with 1/4 meat mixture, 1/4 tomatoes, 1/4 green onions, and 2 tablespoons cheese each. Microwave on high for 1  2 minutes until cheese melts.

Step 5: Top each potato with avocado and 1 tablespoon sour cream.

recipes.label: Butter-Bread
ingredients: 1 gram fresh yeast (available in chilled section of supermarket or from bakery)
recipes.instructions:
Step 1: The night before you wish to make the bread, mix the starter dough ingredients together, cover and put in the fridge overnight. Take the butter and starter dough out of the fridge one hour before starting to make the bread, to bring it to room temperature.

Step 2: Mix the yeast, malt extract, and water together until there are no lumps left. Then tip in the flour, salt, and butter and mix well.

Step 3: Mix in the starter dough mixture and knead for 10 minutes. As this is quite a wet dough it is best to this in a food processor (with the dough hook attachment), 5 minutes on a slow speed then 5 on a fast speed.Tip the dough out onto a floured surface, dust with flour, cover, and leave for 30 minutes to rise. You will need quite a lo of flour for dusting as it is quite sticky. At 10 minute intervals, use your hands to punch flat the dough, stretch out and fold over the edges on themselves and turn over.Grease the loaf tin. Shaking off any excess flour, form a loaf shape then put the dough in the tin. Cover and leave to rise for a further 30 minutes. After 10 minutes rising, use a very sharp knife to score a 2 cm line down the centre of the dough. This helps create a nice crust.Preheat the oven to 230C.

Step 4: Put the bread on the middle shelf of the oven. Using the spray bottle, squirt on and around the bread to create steam. This keeps the bread moist and gives a nice crust.

Step 5: Bake for 45 minutes. After 15 minutes, spray again with water and turn the temperature down to 200C.The bread is ready when a skewer inserted into the centre of the bread comes out clean. Difficult as it may be, leave the bread to cool completely on wire rack before slicing.
